question.label: Political conversations
question.name: In those political conversations, what feels hardest to navigate?
question.description: Drills into the specific dynamic that makes him retreat from senior-level influence into execution.

answer: I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.

assessment.note: He names a specific senior-level gap: hesitation around shaping cross-org alignment, which is a strong clarity signal; [THREAD-DEFERRED] cross-org alignment hesitation worth picking up next session.

new_queue.label: Stretch now
new_queue.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?

new_queue.label: Role in practice
new_queue.name: What feels most solid in your role right now?

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Next-level meaning
new_queue.name: What does senior scope mean to you in practice?

new_queue.label: Ownership boundary
new_queue.name: What are you still carrying personally that should live with the team?

new_queue.label: Manager leverage
new_queue.name: Where would a sharper read from me help you move faster?

new_queue.label: First growth move
new_queue.name: What is the first shift you want to make before we meet again?

history.answer_excerpt: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
